app.name: GitButler
app.one_liner: A modern Git-based version control interface with both GUI and CLI built for AI-powered workflows.

screens.name: Main Workspace
screens.purpose: Display repository state with file diffs, commit history, and branch stack visualization.

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: Configure appearance, AI integrations, and project-specific behaviors like Husky hooks.

rust_commands.name: but_rub
rust_commands.purpose: Address uncommitted hunks by index for AI-assisted code refinement.

rust_commands.name: but_skill_install
rust_commands.purpose: Install or update AI skill files for enhanced Claude Code and Codex performance.

rust_commands.name: but_stack
rust_commands.purpose: Move branches on top of others to create stacked branch workflows.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build basic Tauri/Svelte GUI shell
week 1: Expose `but stack` and `but rub` CLI commands
week 1: Implement core Rust Git abstraction layer
acceptance_tests: File diffs render correctly in floating window when popped out from main view.
Operations log shows auto-commit events and supports undo to prior snapshot.
User can run `but stack` to reorder branches without manual rebase.
